source.catalog_ref: HV22 (user-assigned collegiate combat-robot league; catalog HV21)

full_prompt: Design a monolithic UHMW-PE backed titanium (Ti-6Al-4V) frontal armor wedge plate for a National Havoc Robot League (collegiate combat-robot league) 12 lb (5.44 kg) combat robot class entry, per collegiate combat-robot league 2026 rules for weight cap, LiPo max energy, pneumatic pressure cap, and spinner kinetic-energy cap. The armor is the primary frontal impact face intended to survive repeated strikes from opposing vertical and horizontal spinners delivering up to the collegiate combat-robot league 12 lb class spinner-KE envelope cap (approximately 1500 J single-strike via a class-legal weapon at 300 Hz tooth frequency). The armor is a single monolithic 3D-printed Ti-6Al-4V EBM + machined part (no welds, no riveted-on skin; integral wedge face + integral chassis-mounting flange + integral spinner-gap skirt). For this prompt, the UHMW-PE backer is modeled as a distributed elastic-plastic pad boundary condition on the rear face; the designed part is the monolithic titanium plate alone. Geometry: frontal wedge armor face 200 mm wide x 120 mm tall x 4 mm thick, angled at 30 deg forward rake from vertical to deflect spinner bites downward into the arena floor. Two integral chassis-mounting flanges extending rearward from the wedge top-edge: each 30 mm wide x 40 mm long x 4 mm thick with 2 M5 (5.5 mm clearance) bolt holes at 25 mm pitch. Integral spinner-gap skirt extending downward from the wedge bottom-edge: 200 mm wide x 20 mm tall x 4 mm thick angled 20 deg rearward to close the floor gap. Overall bounding envelope 200 x 120 x 70 mm. Material Ti-6Al-4V (EBM annealed grade 5): E 113 GPa, nu 0.32, density 4430 kg/m^3, yield 830 MPa, UTS 900 MPa, elongation 10%, Charpy V-notch 23 J at 20 C, fatigue endurance 510 MPa at 1e7. Three load cases per collegiate combat-robot league 12 lb armor survival practice. Load Case 1 (repeated vertical-spinner strike on wedge face): 12 kN peak contact force applied normal to the wedge face as a 500 microsecond impulse (representing a 12 lb class class-legal vertical-spinner tooth at 150 m/s tip speed, elastic-plastic contact into the UHMW-PE backer), impacts occur 50 times in a 3-minute match; chassis-mounting flanges fixed to a rigid chassis. Load Case 2 (glancing horizontal-spinner bite along the wedge-skirt intersection): 8 kN tangential + 4 kN normal at the wedge-bottom intersection line, representing a horizontal spinner tooth skimming underneath and biting the skirt transition; superposition with the spinner still engaged laterally. Load Case 3 (hammer-saw catastrophic hit on the wedge face flat): 20 kN peak contact concentrated on a 10 mm x 10 mm patch at the wedge face center, representing a close-range hammer or drum-saw strike; this is a fault-case but armor must not fracture through. Pass/fail: (R1) under LC1 repeated vertical-spinner strike, peak von Mises in the titanium must not exceed 415 MPa (yield/2.0 Ti-6Al-4V); (R2) under LC2 glancing horizontal bite, peak stress must not exceed 553 MPa (yield/1.5 transient); (R3) under LC3 hammer catastrophic hit, peak principal stress must not exceed UTS 900 MPa (fault-case allowed plastic deformation, no through-perforation); (R4) fatigue: 50 strikes per match x 10 matches in a tournament = 500 cycles of LC1 spectrum, local stress range at the wedge-flange fillet must remain below 510 MPa endurance (1e7 margin over 500 cycles); (R5) maximum dent depth at the wedge center under LC1 must not exceed 2 mm (to preserve wedge geometry between strikes - measured as out-of-plane permanent deformation); (R6) armor plate + skirt + flanges mass must not exceed 0.65 kg (allocates ~12% of 5.44 kg 12 lb budget to frontal armor, leaving room for drive + weapon + electronics + rest-of-chassis); (R7) first six natural frequencies (armor + chassis-attached boundary) must exceed 300 Hz to decouple from in-chassis LiPo-driven brushless motor noise 50-150 Hz and pneumatic solenoid valve tick 80 Hz (collegiate combat-robot league pneumatic is capped at 250 psi so valve noise is bounded). Thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses are not required. Deliver STEP AP242 geometry plus report.
short_prompt:
Design a monolithic Ti-6Al-4V (EBM + machined) frontal armor wedge plate for a National Havoc Robot League collegiate combat-robot entry in the 12 lb / 5.44 kg class, backed by a UHMW-PE pad modeled as a distributed elastic-plastic boundary condition on the rear face. The titanium part is a single 3D-printed body with a 30-degree forward-raked wedge face, two rearward chassis-mounting flanges with M5 holes, and a 20-degree rearward-angled lower spinner-gap skirt; no welds, no rivets. Apply yield/2.0 continuous and yield/1.5 transient safety factors, with an UTS principal-stress gate on the catastrophic fault case.

Envelope: 200 x 120 x 70 mm.

Load cases.
- LC1 repeated vertical-spinner strike on the wedge face: 12 kN peak contact over 500 us (class-legal tooth at ~150 m/s), 50 strikes/match, chassis flanges fixed.
- LC2 glancing horizontal-spinner bite at the wedge/skirt intersection: 8 kN tangential + 4 kN normal along the bottom edge, superposed.
- LC3 hammer-saw hit on wedge face: 20 kN on a 10 x 10 mm patch at the face center (fault case, no through-perforation).
Also require permanent dent depth under LC1 below 2 mm, a 500-cycle fatigue margin at flange fillets, and first six modes >300 Hz (decouple 50-150 Hz brushless and 80 Hz pneumatic tick).

prompt.title: collegiate combat-robot league 12 lb class titanium wedge armor plate
prompt.brief: Ti-6Al-4V monolithic wedge 200x120x4 with 30 deg rake + chassis flanges + skirt; 12 kN spinner impact survival.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: second-order tetrahedral (TET10) + UHMW-PE backer contact BC

eval_coverage: collegiate combat-robot league 12 lb weight budget allocation
